title: Neither US Exceptionalism Nor A European Boom
text:
Neither US Exceptionalism Nor A European Boom

Cedric Gemehl, Tan Kai Xian

Gavekal Research | 20 Jul 2026

Recent volatility in US technology stocks has reignited the debate over US exceptionalism. From an equity market perspective, the discussion is often framed as a binary choice. Proponents argue US equities will continue to outperform those in the rest of the world, while skeptics contend that the era of US exceptionalism is coming to an end and that equities elsewhere are now poised to outperform their US counterparts. The debate over Europe’s outlook is framed in similarly binary terms.A comparison of the relative performance of US and European equities offers an interesting insight. As of July 17, 2026, the ratio of the MSCI US index to the MSCI Europe index, measured on a total-return-basis in US dollar terms, stood exactly at its early March 2022 level. On an equally-weighted-basis, the same ratio stood at its early October 2021 level.
